title: React.memo – Memoizing Components
description: Prevent re‑renders when props haven't changed.

slides.title: Memoization for Components 📦
slides.content:
# React.memo

`React.memo` is a higher‑order component that memoizes the result. If a component renders the same result given the same props, wrapping it in `React.memo` tells React to skip re‑rendering if its props haven't changed.

## Syntax

```jsx
const MemoizedComponent = React.memo(MyComponent);
```

Now, `MemoizedComponent` will only re‑render when its props change.

## When to Use

* Pure functional components that render often with the same props.
* Components that are expensive to render.

## Shallow Comparison

`React.memo` does a shallow comparison of props. For complex objects, you can provide a custom comparison function as the second argument.

```jsx
function areEqual(prevProps, nextProps) {
  // return true if props are equal (skip re‑render)
}
```

slides.title: React.memo in Action
slides.content: Without memo, the child re‑renders every time the parent re‑renders. With memo, it re‑renders only when its specific props change.

quizData.question: React.memo performs a deep comparison of props by default.
quizData.explanation: False, it performs a shallow comparison. For deep equality, you need to provide a custom comparison function.
